problem.title: Decode Ways
problem.description: A message containing letters from A-Z can be encoded into numbers using the following mapping: 'A' -> '1', 'B' -> '2', ..., 'Z' -> '26'.
assumptions: Return number of ways to decode the string.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Dynamic Programming
solutions.description: Build a DP array where dp[i] is the number of ways to decode the first i characters. At each position, add dp[i-1] if the current single digit is valid (1-9), and add dp[i-2] if the two-digit number formed with the previous character is valid (10-26). A leading '0' makes the single-digit decode invalid, and this is handled by the range checks.
